description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, alternating between a light, speckled stone texture and dark, recessed channels. The sides of the block are solid and smooth, with a consistent dark coloration, suggesting a dense, robust material. The top surface appears to be made of a polished or semi-polished granite or similar stone, with visible speckles and a slight sheen. The block is thick and heavy, likely intended as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, such as a sidewalk or plaza. There are no visible engravings, markings, or signs of significant weathering, giving it a modern and utilitarian appearance.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with parallel grooves on its speckled top surface and solid dark sides, resembling a modern urban barrier.
description_view_0: Front view showing the striped, grooved top surface and the solid, dark front face.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the grooves and the contrast between the light stone and dark channels.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the grooved pattern and solid base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the deep grooves and alternating color pattern.
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
